title: stylidium emarginatum
intro: Stylidium emarginatum, commonly known as the spiky triggerplant, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to Western Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Stylidium emarginatum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and even exhibit antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of this plant may include infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
